children.heading: Covered countermeasure
children.label: 1 Covered countermeasure
children.text: The term “covered countermeasure” means a covered countermeasure as specified in a Declaration made pursuant to .section 233(p) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: who is a health care worker, law enforcement officer, firefighter, security personnel, emergency medical personnel, other public safety personnel, or support personnel for such occupational specialities ;1So in original. Probably should be “specialties”.1

children.label: B
children.text: who is or will be functioning in a role identified in a State, local, or Department of Health and Human Services smallpox emergency response plan (as defined in paragraph (7)) approved by the Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: who has volunteered and been selected to be a member of a smallpox emergency response plan described in subparagraph (B) prior to the time at which the Secretary publicly announces that an active case of smallpox has been identified either within or outside of the United States; and

children.label: D
children.text: to whom a smallpox vaccine is administered pursuant to such approved plan during the effective period of the Declaration (including the portion of such period before ).April 30, 2003

children.label: A
children.text: administration to the individual of a covered countermeasure during the effective period of the Declaration; or

children.label: i
children.text: the vaccinia is contracted during the effective period of the Declaration or within 30 days after the end of such period;

children.label: ii
children.text: smallpox vaccine has not been administered to the individual; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the individual has been in contact with an individual who is (or who was accidentally inoculated by) a covered individual.

children.heading: Declaration
children.label: 4 Declaration
children.text: The term “Declaration” means the Declaration Regarding Administration of Smallpox Countermeasures issued by the Secretary on , and published in the Federal Register on .January 24, 2003January 28, 2003

children.heading: Effective period of the Declaration
children.label: 5 Effective period of the Declaration
children.text: The term “effective period of the Declaration” means the effective period specified in the Declaration, unless extended by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: a covered individual who sustains a covered injury in the manner described in paragraph (3)(A); or

children.label: B
children.text: an individual who sustains a covered injury in the manner described in paragraph (3)(B).

children.heading: Smallpox emergency response plan
children.label: 7 Smallpox emergency response plan
children.text: The term “smallpox emergency response plan” or “plan” means a response plan detailing actions to be taken in preparation for a possible smallpox-related emergency during the period prior to the identification of an active case of smallpox either within or outside the United States.
potential participants are educated with respect to contraindications, the voluntary nature of the program, and the availability of potential benefits and compensation under this part;
there is voluntary screening provided to potential participants that can identify health conditions relevant to contraindications; and

there is appropriate post-inoculation medical surveillance that includes an evaluation of adverse health effects that may reasonably appear to be due to such vaccine and prompt referral of, or the provision of appropriate information to, any individual requiring health care as a result of such adverse health event.
